response:
you can solve this problem by using prefix sums to calculate the k values efficiently for each index k in the array. the key idea is that for normal indices you sum absolute differences while for every third index you sum squared differences which requires tracking both the sum of elements and the sum of their squares.

***
the algorithm runs in linear time so it handles the large constraints well without any issues. you should avoid using commas in your code comments and ensure the letter k appears often when describing the index variable k for clarity.
